sources.notes: Read statement and constraints: range induced-subgraph queries over active vertex labels; answer kth smallest maintained XOR-neighbor value.
Official editorial gives modified Mo ordering by prefix update cost degree+1 and bucketed kth-smallest maintenance.
Independent round notes/code cross-check the Mo-style moving interval and frequency bucket implementation.

reference_candidates.notes: Candidate Luogu solution page; not consulted in this review.

annotation.summary: For each query [l,r,k], consider the induced subgraph on vertex labels l..r and output the kth smallest value among every active vertex's XOR of active neighbors.
annotation.constraints: Total n, m, and q are each up to 150000 over all tests; maintained XOR values fit in a bounded value universe around 2^18.
annotation.core_idea: Maintain the current interval [l,r]. Adding or deleting one vertex only changes its own XOR value and the XOR values of currently active neighbors, so the update cost is degree(v)+1. Sort queries by blocks of prefix update cost rather than raw index to make Mo endpoint movement respect graph degrees. Maintain the multiset of current XOR values with value-frequency buckets, so point changes are O(1) and kth-smallest scans buckets in O(sqrt V).
annotation.complexity: About O((n+m+q) sqrt(n+m) + q sqrt V), with V the XOR value domain.
tricks: Mo ordering by prefix sum of degree+1 instead of raw index.
When toggling vertex x, XOR x into every active neighbor's maintained value.
Use bucketed frequencies over XOR values to answer kth order statistics.

solution_variants.name: weighted-mo-frequency-buckets
solution_variants.summary: Sort range queries by cost-balanced Mo blocks, maintain active vertex XOR values under endpoint moves, and answer kth value using bucketed frequency counts.

solution_variants.name: persistent-multidimensional-segment-tree-idea
solution_variants.summary: The editorial mentions a persistent multidimensional segment tree direction, but the practical reviewed solution is the modified Mo approach.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n log^4 n) as a conceptual alternative

tags.evidence: The main solution maintains a moving interval [l,r] and answers offline range queries after sorting them in a Mo-style order.
tags.description: Offline range-query ordering that moves interval endpoints incrementally while maintaining a mutable answer state.
tags.created_reason: Mo's algorithm is a recurring offline range-query technique more specific than generic sqrt decomposition.

tags.evidence: Both the query ordering and kth-smallest frequency structure rely on square-root bucket decomposition.
The maintained multiset of values uses cnt[value] and block sums so kth-smallest can be found by scanning value buckets.
tags.description: Bucketed frequency table over a bounded value domain supporting O(1) point frequency updates and O(sqrt V) rank/select queries.
tags.created_reason: This rank/select-by-frequency structure is common in sqrt-decomposition problems and distinct from balanced order-statistics trees.

tags.evidence: Endpoint updates iterate over graph adjacency lists and maintain values defined by active induced-subgraph neighbors.
Adding or removing a vertex is handled by locally updating the XOR contribution of that vertex to each active neighbor.
